question: A custom sync validator function should return what values?
options: true when valid, false when invalid
null when valid, a ValidationErrors object (plain object) when invalid
An empty string when valid, an error message string when invalid
undefined when valid, an Error instance when invalid

question: Where do you apply a cross-field validator that compares 'password' and 'confirmPassword'?
options: On the 'confirmPassword' FormControl as a sync validator
On the 'password' FormControl as a sync validator
On the parent FormGroup using the validators option — group-level validators receive the group as the AbstractControl
As a template directive on the <form> element

question: An async validator checks username availability via an API. Without any extra config, when does it fire?
options: Only when the form is submitted
Only when the control loses focus (blur)
On every value change — every keystroke triggers the async validator
Once when the component initializes

question: How do you add a new row to a FormArray at runtime?
options: this.formArray.controls.push(newControl) — push directly to the controls array
this.formArray.push(newControl) — use the FormArray.push() method
this.form.patchValue({ arrayField: [...existing, newControl] })
You cannot add controls to a FormArray after it is created

question: What does `updateOn: 'blur'` do when applied to a FormControl?
options: It makes the control only show validation errors after the field loses focus
It changes when Angular updates the control's value and re-runs validators — from on every change to on blur only
It disables the control until the user has focused and blurred it
It runs async validators immediately when the field gains focus
